reasoning:
The threshold temperature based on the 2025-10-26 value from the dataset is 9.9625 °C. To evaluate the probabilities for the later dates, I analyzed the climatology of Saint-Girons (Station 07627, located in the Pyrenees foothills). Due to its location, Saint-Girons experiences significant temperature variance, including frequent winter Foehn winds that can lead to unusually warm days, although the season-average temperatures decline deeply into winter.

1. For 2025-11-02: Early November sees a climatological normal daily mean slightly above 10 °C (around 10.5 - 11 °C with current warming trends). Given relatively high daily variance, the probability of exceeding the ~9.96 °C threshold is slightly favorbale, around 61%.
2. For 2025-11-25: Late November is colder, with the climatological mean daily temperature dropping to roughly 7.5 - 8.5 °C. Surpassing 10 °C is still quite plausible during anomalous mild flows, but less than an even bet, lowering the probability to roughly 39%.
3. For 2026-01-24: Deep winter (late January) generally has an average temperature near 5 - 6 °C. While exceeding 9.9625 °C would require an anomalously mild day (e.g., a Foehn-effect southerly flow), historical data in DBnomics (e.g., 2024-01-25 at 11.2 °C and 2023-12-11 at 11.0 °C) confirms this is entirely possible, though historically accounting for only roughly the warmest 20-30% of winter days. As this is substantially driven by seasonal trends pulling the base rate down, the objective probability should decrease to circa 29%.
batch_queries: What is the probability that the daily average temperature at the French weather station at Saint-Girons will be higher on each resolution date listed below than on 2025-10-26?
